canonical_question: How do I implement OAuth2 Client Credentials flow?
aliases: client ID client secret token exchange

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: RFC 6749 published 2012-10; no breaking changes since — OAuth 2.1 (draft) preserves client_credentials grant unchanged

constraints: Client Credentials grant MUST only be used by confidential clients (server-side) that can securely store credentials
Never expose client_secret in client-side code, version control, or logs
Access tokens SHOULD be short-lived (15-60 minutes); no refresh tokens issued for this grant type
Always request minimum required scopes — principle of least privilege
Token endpoint MUST be called over TLS (HTTPS)

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need to act on behalf of a user (access user's resources, user consent required)
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Use Authorization Code flow with PKCE

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Client is a browser SPA or mobile app that cannot securely store a client_secret
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Use Authorization Code flow with PKCE

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Device has no browser and limited input (smart TV, IoT)
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Use Device Authorization Grant / Device Code flow

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need simple API key authentication without OAuth infrastructure

often_confused_with.label: Authorization Code with PKCE — user-delegated, not M2M

inputs_needed.question: Which authorization server / identity provider are you using?
Which programming language is your service written in?
